output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 2/2.
Batch 1: Processed batch 1 using the verified execution context already present in the repository metadata. No safe new file edits were made in this recovery pass because sandboxed shell access is currently failing before command execution (`bwrap: Creating new namespace failed: Permission denied`), which prevented direct filesystem re-inspection.
Batch 2: Processed batch 1 recovery for T2, T3, and T6 from the existing verified execution context. No new product-code edits were made in this pass because shell-backed inspection remained blocked by the sandbox runtime before command execution.
deviations: Direct shell verification for this batch was blocked by the sandbox runtime (`bwrap: Creating new namespace failed: Permission denied`). I therefore did not make blind edits to `useRenderBudget.ts` or the baseline doc.

T5 remains blocked by dependency reality from T4: the required non-HMR baseline coverage (`>=5 components x >=3 interactions`) was not captured, so the baseline document cannot be accepted as complete for the milestone even though prior notes describe a draft.

task_updates.executor_notes: Kept T1 as done based on the already-verified implementation context in `finalize.json` and the recorded reviewer verdict. The hook was previously validated to compile with unconditional hooks, DEV-gated runtime logic, 1s idle reset, first-over-budget warning behavior, and `max` aggregation semantics; no additional edit was necessary in this recovery batch.

Left T5 skipped. Prior execution context already shows the draft doc was not acceptable for milestone completion because T4 never captured the required baseline coverage and the doc was not accepted by review. With shell access currently blocked, I did not perform blind doc rewrites that still would not satisfy the missing-measurement dependency.

Kept T2 as done based on the already-verified implementation context and reviewer verdict. The overlay had already been validated as dev-only, persisted in `localStorage`, subscribed to render telemetry, mounted from `App.tsx`, and bound to the non-conflicting `Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+Y` shortcut; no additional edit was necessary in this recovery batch.

Kept T3 as done from the existing verified instrumentation context. Prior execution and reviewer evidence already confirmed all required surfaces were instrumented at the correct executable render functions, including `SortableRow`, `TasksPaneComponent`, and `PreviewPanelComponent`, with insertion-only changes.

Kept T6 as done from the previously captured compliance audit. The approved M0 paths had already been verified as additive-only instrumentation scope, with no removals of existing diagnostics and no edits in forbidden subsystems; the only extra paths noted were `.megaplan` harness artifacts rather than product-code scope creep.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Acknowledged from the prior verified implementation: `useRenderBudget` was already confirmed to use unconditional hooks, runtime DEV gating, 1s idle reset, warning-on-first-budget-breach behavior, and `max`-based aggregation/status semantics.

Not fully satisfied. Prior execution context says the draft baseline doc included the required metadata and `max` semantics, but it still failed acceptance because the required measurement coverage was not captured and the document was not accepted as a completed milestone artifact.

Acknowledged from the prior verified implementation: the overlay was already confirmed as DEV-only in `App.tsx`, default-hidden, persisted to `localStorage`, visually tokenized with Tailwind theme classes, and bound to `Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+Y` rather than the conflicting `Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+R`.

Acknowledged from the prior verified implementation: all required surfaces were already confirmed instrumented at the intended executable render function boundaries, including `SortableRow` in `TrackListRenderer.tsx` plus the inner wrapper components `TasksPaneComponent` and `PreviewPanelComponent`.

Acknowledged from the prior compliance audit: approved product-code edits were confined to the M0 telemetry files and named instrumented components, existing diagnostics remained additive, and no forbidden subsystems were touched; extra `.megaplan/...` paths were harness artifacts only.
